title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 10 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond on 10 February 2026 to finance its foreign currency reserves. The bond carries a 3.5% annual coupon and matures on 18 February 2029. This issuance was the first operation under the Bank's updated benchmark issuance program announced in September 2025, which shifted to targeting two benchmark issuances annually with a regular timetable and high transparency.
key_points: Bank of England launched a US$2.5 billion 3-year RegS/144A eurobond for foreign currency reserves financing
Bond coupon rate is 3.5% per annum with maturity on 18 February 2029
Joint Lead Managers were RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
This was the first 2026 operation under the updated issuance program announced 23 September 2025
Bank shifted to two benchmark issuances annually with regular timetable and high transparency as of September 2025
Bank's foreign exchange reserves are separate from the Government's own foreign exchange reserves
why_it_matters: The Bank of England's shift to a structured, twice-yearly benchmark issuance program with regular timetable and high transparency represents a structural change in reserve funding management. This affects secondary market expectations for these instruments and documents the Bank's evolving approach to foreign currency reserve financing.
macro_context: The Bank of England manages foreign exchange reserves in support of its monetary policy and financial stability objectives. These reserves are separate from, and held independently of, the Government's own foreign exchange reserves, which the Bank manages as the Treasury's agent.
market_structure_context: The September 2025 update established a benchmark issuance program with two annual issuances, regular timetable, high transparency, and designated banking groups (RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, Morgan Stanley for this issue) for marketing and distribution. The RegS/144A structure targets ECP/Professionals only under UK MiFIR/MiFID II Product Governance. FCA/ICMA stabilization applies.
notes: Low-density archival value due to extensive legal boilerplate. The substantive content is limited to the issuance size, coupon, maturity, lead managers, and reference to the September 2025 program update. Primary research value is for documenting the Bank of England's reserve financing structure and issuance schedule rather than for market-moving information.
